steps.title: Identify the sampling population
steps.content: The problem explicitly states the sample was selected at random from all high school students in the district. The fundamental principle of sampling is that results can be generalized only to the population from which the random sample was drawn.

steps.title: Find the largest population to which results generalize
steps.content:
• B: The 353 surveyed students is the sample itself, not a population to generalize to.
  • C: The 300 who didn't approve is observed data within the sample, not a population.
  • D: All high school students in the state is too broad—the sample comes only from the district and may not represent the full state.
  • A: All high school students in the district is the population from which the sample was drawn.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
